# Research: stripe-subscription-vs-one-time-lifecycle

*Auto-generated by AIDLC planning session*

**Question:** How does Stripe model a one-time purchase (single pool plan) vs an annual subscription in terms of webhook events, customer objects, and entitlement expiry? What events drive renewal, cancellation, and refund handling, and how should these map to the internal Purchase/Entitlement data model?

---

# Stripe: One-Time Purchase vs Annual Subscription — Lifecycle & Data Model

## Overview

Stripe has two fundamentally different payment primitives:

| Dimension | One-Time (Payment Intent) | Annual Subscription |
|---|---|---|
| Core object | `PaymentIntent` → `Charge` | `Subscription` → recurring `Invoice` → `PaymentIntent` → `Charge` |
| Customer required? | Optional (recommended) | Required |
| Renewal | Manual / none | Automatic |
| Entitlement signal | `payment_intent.succeeded` | `invoice.paid` (recurring) |
| Cancellation | N/A post-payment | `customer.subscription.deleted` |
| Refund | `charge.refund.created` | `charge.refund.created` + proration credit |

---

## 1. Customer Object

### One-Time Purchase
- A `Customer` object is **optional** but strongly recommended so you can attach payment methods, issue refunds, and link orders to an identity.
- If you skip creating a customer, the `PaymentIntent` is anonymous. You lose the ability to query all charges for a user.
- Recommended flow: create or retrieve `Customer` by your internal `user_id` before creating the `PaymentIntent`.

```
Customer
  id: cus_xxx
  email: user@example.com
  metadata:
    internal_user_id: "usr_123"
```

### Annual Subscription
- A `Customer` is **required** — subscriptions are always attached to a customer.
- The customer holds the default payment method that Stripe charges automatically each billing cycle.
- Stripe creates a `Subscription` object linked to the customer with a `Price` (recurring interval = `year`).

```
Customer
  id: cus_xxx
  default_source / default_payment_method: pm_xxx
  subscriptions: [sub_xxx]
```

---

## 2. Core Object Hierarchy

### One-Time Purchase Path

```
Customer (cus_xxx)
  └── PaymentIntent (pi_xxx)
        ├── status: requires_payment_method → processing → succeeded
        └── Charge (ch_xxx)
              ├── amount: 4999 (cents)
              ├── paid: true
              └── Refund (re_xxx)  ← created on refund
```

### Annual Subscription Path

```
Customer (cus_xxx)
  └── Subscription (sub_xxx)
        ├── status: trialing | active | past_due | canceled | unpaid
        ├── current_period_start / current_period_end
        ├── cancel_at_period_end: bool
        └── Invoice (in_xxx)   ← one per billing cycle
              ├── status: draft → open → paid | void
              ├── subscription: sub_xxx
              └── PaymentIntent (pi_xxx)
                    └── Charge (ch_xxx)
                          └── Refund (re_xxx)
```

Key subscription fields to store:

| Field | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `sub_xxx.current_period_end` | Unix timestamp — entitlement expiry for this cycle |
| `sub_xxx.cancel_at_period_end` | True when user cancels but access continues until period end |
| `sub_xxx.status` | Drives access gate: `active` or `trialing` = access granted |
| `sub_xxx.items[].price.id` | Which plan/price the user is on |

---

## 3. Webhook Events by Scenario

### 3.1 One-Time Purchase — Happy Path

```
payment_intent.created          ← intent created server-side
payment_intent.processing       ← card network processing (async methods)
payment_intent.succeeded        ← GRANT ENTITLEMENT HERE
charge.succeeded                ← accompanies succeeded PI
```

**Action on `payment_intent.succeeded`:**
- Verify `metadata.internal_user_id` and `metadata.plan_id`
- Create `Purchase` record
- Create `Entitlement` record with `expires_at = NOW() + plan_duration` (or `NULL` for lifetime)

### 3.2 One-Time Purchase — Failure

```
payment_intent.payment_failed   ← do NOT grant entitlement
charge.failed                   ← log for diagnostics
```

### 3.3 Annual Subscription — Initial Purchase

```
customer.subscription.created   ← subscription object exists (status may be incomplete)
invoice.created                 ← draft invoice for first cycle
invoice.finalized               ← invoice locked
invoice.payment_succeeded       ← payment succeeded
invoice.paid                    ← GRANT/RENEW ENTITLEMENT HERE (canonical event)
payment_intent.succeeded        ← sub-event, also fires
customer.subscription.updated   ← status transitions to "active"
```

**Use `invoice.paid` as your canonical renewal signal** — it fires for both the first charge and every subsequent renewal, giving a single handler for initial + renewal.

### 3.4 Annual Subscription — Renewal (Year 2+)

```
invoice.upcoming                ← fires ~1 week before renewal (informational)
invoice.created
invoice.finalized
invoice.payment_succeeded
invoice.paid                    ← extend entitlement: expires_at = invoice.lines[].period.end
customer.subscription.updated   ← current_period_start/end updated
```

**Action on `invoice.paid` where `invoice.billing_reason = "subscription_cycle"`:**
- Extend `Entitlement.expires_at` to `invoice.lines[0].period.end` (Unix → datetime)
- Record new `Purchase` or `PaymentRecord` linked to the invoice

### 3.5 Annual Subscription — Cancellation (Cancel at Period End)

```
customer.subscription.updated   ← cancel_at_period_end flips to true
```

User retains access until `current_period_end`. No entitlement change yet.

```
customer.subscription.deleted   ← fires AT period end; status = "canceled"
```

**Action on `customer.subscription.deleted`:**
- Set `Entitlement.revoked_at = NOW()` (or let `expires_at` naturally expire — see design note below)
- Optionally send "your access has ended" email

### 3.6 Annual Subscription — Immediate Cancellation

```
customer.subscription.updated   ← status → canceled (if canceled immediately via API)
customer.subscription.deleted
```

No grace period; revoke entitlement immediately.

### 3.7 Payment Failure / Dunning

```
invoice.payment_failed          ← first failure
customer.subscription.updated   ← status → past_due (after retry exhausted)
invoice.payment_failed          ← retry 2, 3... (per Smart Retries config)
customer.subscription.updated   ← status → unpaid (if all retries fail)
customer.subscription.deleted   ← if configured to cancel after failure
```

**Action on `customer.subscription.updated` where `status = "past_due"`:**
- Optionally restrict access (grace period policy is your call)
- Send dunning email

**Action on `customer.subscription.deleted` after dunning:**
- Revoke entitlement

### 3.8 Refund — One-Time Purchase

```
charge.refund.created           ← Refund object attached to Charge
charge.refunded                 ← charge.refunded = true (full refund)
```

**Action:**
- Revoke `Entitlement` immediately
- Record `refund_id`, `refund_amount`, `refunded_at` on `Purchase`

### 3.9 Refund — Subscription (Prorated Credit)

```
credit_note.created             ← Stripe creates a credit note for unused time
charge.refund.created           ← only if you issue a cash refund via API
customer.subscription.updated   ← if also canceling
customer.subscription.deleted   ← if also canceling
```

Proration credits can be applied to future invoices (default) or refunded to original payment method — this is a Stripe Dashboard / API setting.

---

## 4. Idempotency and Event Ordering

Stripe guarantees **at-least-once** delivery. Your handlers must be idempotent:

- Key on `stripe_event_id` or the object ID + event type
- For `invoice.paid`: key on `invoice.id` — idempotent upsert of the entitlement record
- For `payment_intent.succeeded` on a subscription invoice: the parent `invoice.paid` is the canonical event; skip duplicate processing if an invoice exists

Event ordering is **not guaranteed**. Do not assume `invoice.created` arrives before `invoice.paid`. Design each handler to be self-sufficient by fetching the full object from Stripe if needed.

---

## 5. Internal Data Model

### 5.1 Tables

```sql
-- Represents the Stripe customer linkage
CREATE TABLE stripe_customers (
  id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id         UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
  stripe_cus_id   TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,           -- cus_xxx
  created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- A completed payment event (one row per PaymentIntent or Invoice)
CREATE TABLE purchases (
  id                  UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id             UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
  stripe_cus_id       TEXT NOT NULL,
  stripe_pi_id        TEXT,                        -- pi_xxx (one-time or sub invoice PI)
  stripe_invoice_id   TEXT,                        -- in_xxx (subscription invoices)
  stripe_sub_id       TEXT,                        -- sub_xxx (null for one-time)
  plan_id             TEXT NOT NULL,               -- your internal plan identifier
  amount_cents        INT NOT NULL,
  currency            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'usd',
  status              TEXT NOT NULL,               -- succeeded | refunded | failed
  refund_id           TEXT,                        -- re_xxx
  refunded_at         TIMESTAMPTZ,
  stripe_event_id     TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,        -- idempotency key
  created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Active access grant
CREATE TABLE entitlements (
  id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id         UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
  purchase_id     UUID REFERENCES purchases(id),
  plan_id         TEXT NOT NULL,
  stripe_sub_id   TEXT,                            -- null for one-time
  status          TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active',  -- active | expired | revoked
  granted_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  expires_at      TIMESTAMPTZ,                     -- null = lifetime
  revoked_at      TIMESTAMPTZ,
  updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Raw Stripe events for audit / replay
CREATE TABLE stripe_events (
  stripe_event_id   TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  event_type        TEXT NOT NULL,
  payload           JSONB NOT NULL,
  processed_at      TIMESTAMPTZ,
  error             TEXT,
  created_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

### 5.2 Access Check Query

```sql
SELECT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 FROM entitlements
  WHERE user_id = $1
    AND plan_id = $2
    AND status = 'active'
    AND (expires_at IS NULL OR expires_at > NOW())
    AND revoked_at IS NULL
) AS has_access;
```

---

## 6. Handler Logic by Event

### `payment_intent.succeeded` (one-time purchase only)

```python
def handle_pi_succeeded(event):
    pi = event["data"]["object"]

    # Skip if this PI belongs to a subscription invoice — invoice.paid handles it
    if pi.get("invoice"):
        return

    user_id = pi["metadata"]["internal_user_id"]
    plan_id = pi["metadata"]["plan_id"]

    purchase = upsert_purchase(
        stripe_event_id=event["id"],
        stripe_pi_id=pi["id"],
        user_id=user_id,
        plan_id=plan_id,
        amount_cents=pi["amount"],
        status="succeeded",
    )

    plan = get_plan(plan_id)
    expires_at = None if plan.lifetime else utcnow() + plan.duration

    upsert_entitlement(
        user_id=user_id,
        purchase_id=purchase.id,
        plan_id=plan_id,
        expires_at=expires_at,
    )
```

### `invoice.paid` (subscriptions — initial + renewal)

```python
def handle_invoice_paid(event):
    inv = event["data"]["object"]
    sub_id = inv["subscription"]
    if not sub_id:
        return  # not a subscription invoice

    sub = stripe.Subscription.retrieve(sub_id)
    user_id = sub["metadata"]["internal_user_id"]
    plan_id = sub["metadata"]["plan_id"]

    # Period end from the subscription line item
    line = inv["lines"]["data"][0]
    expires_at = datetime.utcfromtimestamp(line["period"]["end"])

    purchase = upsert_purchase(
        stripe_event_id=event["id"],
        stripe_pi_id=inv.get("payment_intent"),
        stripe_invoice_id=inv["id"],
        stripe_sub_id=sub_id,
        user_id=user_id,
        plan_id=plan_id,
        amount_cents=inv["amount_paid"],
        status="succeeded",
    )

    upsert_entitlement(
        user_id=user_id,
        purchase_id=purchase.id,
        plan_id=plan_id,
        stripe_sub_id=sub_id,
        expires_at=expires_at,   # extended each renewal
        status="active",
        revoked_at=None,
    )
```

### `customer.subscription.deleted`

```python
def handle_sub_deleted(event):
    sub = event["data"]["object"]
    sub_id = sub["id"]

    # Revoke the entitlement linked to this subscription
    revoke_entitlement_by_sub(sub_id=sub_id, revoked_at=utcnow())
    # Optionally trigger "access ended" email
```

### `charge.refund.created`

```python
def handle_refund_created(event):
    refund = event["data"]["object"]
    charge_id = refund["charge"]

    purchase = get_purchase_by_charge(charge_id)
    if not purchase:
        return

    mark_purchase_refunded(
        purchase_id=purchase.id,
        refund_id=refund["id"],
        refunded_at=utcnow(),
    )
    revoke_entitlement(purchase_id=purchase.id, revoked_at=utcnow())
```

---

## 7. Entitlement Expiry Design: `expires_at` vs Event-Driven Revocation

Two schools of thought:

### Option A: Passive expiry via `expires_at` (recommended for subscriptions)
- Set `expires_at = current_period_end` on each renewal
- Access check: `expires_at > NOW()`
- On cancellation (`cancel_at_period_end = true`): do nothing — the existing `expires_at` naturally cuts off access at the right time
- On `customer.subscription.deleted`: optionally set `revoked_at` as belt-and-suspenders, or simply let `expires_at` expire
- **Pro:** No race condition between Stripe firing `deleted` and your handler running; user never loses access prematurely

### Option B: Active revocation via `revoked_at`
- On `customer.subscription.deleted`: immediately set `revoked_at`
- Requires careful timing — if Stripe fires slightly before `current_period_end`, user loses access early
- **Pro:** Explicit audit trail; simpler access query

**Recommended hybrid:** use `expires_at` as the primary gate for subscriptions; use `revoked_at` only for explicit admin revocation or refunds.

---

## 8. Metadata Conventions

Always set metadata on the Stripe objects you create so webhooks can route back to your system without a DB lookup:

```python
# On Customer creation
stripe.Customer.create(
    email=user.email,
    metadata={"internal_user_id": user.id}
)

# On PaymentIntent (one-time)
stripe.PaymentIntent.create(
    amount=price_cents,
    currency="usd",
    customer=cus_id,
    metadata={
        "internal_user_id": user.id,
        "plan_id": "pool_single_2026",
    }
)

# On Subscription creation
stripe.Subscription.create(
    customer=cus_id,
    items=[{"price": price_id}],
    metadata={
        "internal_user_id": user.id,
        "plan_id": "pool_annual_2026",
    }
)
```

---

## 9. Key Differences Summary

| Concern | One-Time | Annual Subscription |
|---|---|---|
| Entitlement trigger | `payment_intent.succeeded` (no invoice) | `invoice.paid` |
| Renewal | Manual re-purchase | Automatic; handled by same `invoice.paid` handler |
| Expiry | Fixed `expires_at` at purchase time | `expires_at` updated each cycle to `period.end` |
| Cancellation webhook | N/A | `customer.subscription.deleted` |
| Refund webhook | `charge.refund.created` | `charge.refund.created` + optional `credit_note.created` |
| Customer required | Recommended | Required |
| Idempotency key | `event.id` keyed on PI ID | `event.id` keyed on invoice ID |
| Dunning handling | N/A | `customer.subscription.updated` status → `past_due` |

---

## 10. Checklist for Implementation

- [ ] Stripe Customer created/retrieved for every purchasing user; `internal_user_id` in metadata
- [ ] `stripe_events` table with `stripe_event_id` UNIQUE — insert before processing for idempotency
- [ ] `payment_intent.succeeded` handler skips PIs with a non-null `invoice` field
- [ ] `invoice.paid` handler covers both initial and renewal for subscriptions
- [ ] `expires_at` set from `invoice.lines[0].period.end`, not `current_period_end` on the subscription (the line item is more precise for prorated cycles)
- [ ] `customer.subscription.deleted` handler revokes or confirms expiry
- [ ] `charge.refund.created` handler revokes entitlement and marks purchase refunded
- [ ] `customer.subscription.updated` handler flags `past_due` for dunning UX
- [ ] Webhook endpoint verifies `Stripe-Signature` header with `stripe.WebhookSignature.verify_header`
- [ ] All handlers return HTTP 200 even on known-duplicate events (idempotent skip)
